requirements.text: `extensions/worktree.ts` establishes the pattern: individual `pi.registerCommand()` calls, shell via `pi.exec("bash", ["-lc", cmd])`, user feedback via `ctx.ui?.notify()`, confirmation via `ctx.ui.confirm()`, selection via `ctx.ui.select()`
requirements.heading: Existing codebase context

requirements.text: PR creation logic already exists in `handleWorktreePr()` (uses `gh pr create`, `gh pr view`) — the new extension needs a simpler, non-worktree-aware version
requirements.heading: Existing codebase context

requirements.text: Package uses `"type": "module"` with peer deps on `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent` and `@mariozechner/pi-ai`
requirements.heading: Existing codebase context

requirements.text: `/git-status` — show `git status --short`, notify result
requirements.heading: Scope

requirements.text: `/git-diff` — show truncated diff (staged + unstaged); optional path argument
requirements.heading: Scope

requirements.text: `/git-checkout <branch>` — switch branch; interactive picker via `ctx.ui.select()` when no argument given; prompt to stash on dirty tree
requirements.heading: Scope

requirements.text: `/git-create-branch <slug>` — create and checkout new branch
requirements.heading: Scope

requirements.text: `/git-remote-main` — `git checkout main && git pull origin main`; prompt to stash on dirty tree
requirements.heading: Scope

requirements.text: `/git-commit <message>` — stage all + commit; if no message argument, prompt via `ctx.ui.input()`
requirements.heading: Scope

requirements.text: `/git-pr` — create PR via `gh pr create` against main; detect existing PR and update instead
requirements.heading: Scope

requirements.text: `/git-pr-update` — push current branch, update existing PR (or notify if none exists)
requirements.heading: Scope

requirements.text: Worktree integration (handled by `extensions/worktree.ts`)
requirements.heading: Out of scope

requirements.text: Rebase / merge conflict resolution
requirements.heading: Out of scope

requirements.text: Git log browsing
requirements.heading: Out of scope

requirements.text: Multi-remote support (assumes `origin`)
requirements.heading: Out of scope

requirements.text: Stash management beyond auto-stash-before-checkout
requirements.heading: Out of scope

requirements.text: **Individual commands**, not a single `/git` dispatcher — matches worktree extension pattern and allows multi-step orchestration per command
requirements.heading: Clarified decisions

requirements.text: **Interactive branch picker** when `/git-checkout` is called without arguments — uses `ctx.ui.select()` with output of `git branch -a`, strips `remotes/origin/` prefixes for display
requirements.heading: Clarified decisions

requirements.text: **Dirty working tree** on checkout/remote-main: prompt user via `ctx.ui.confirm()`, auto-stash if confirmed, auto-unstash after checkout
requirements.heading: Clarified decisions

requirements.text: **`/git-remote-main`** does exactly `checkout main && pull origin main` — no worktree metadata interaction
requirements.heading: Clarified decisions

requirements.text: **PR commands** use `gh` CLI — `ensureGhAuthenticated()` check before PR operations; always target `main` as base branch
requirements.heading: Clarified decisions

requirements.text: **Commit** stages all changes (`git add -A`) before committing — matches the worktree extension's commit behavior
requirements.heading: Clarified decisions

requirements.text: **Duplicate utilities** (`trimOutput`, `shQuote`, `detectRepoRoot`, `ensureGhAuthenticated`) — inline in the new file, no shared module
requirements.heading: Clarified decisions

requirements.text: **Diff truncation** — truncate large diff output in notifications
requirements.heading: Clarified decisions

requirements.text: `origin` is the single relevant remote
requirements.heading: Assumptions

requirements.text: `main` is the default branch name
requirements.heading: Assumptions

requirements.text: `gh` CLI is available and authenticated for PR commands
requirements.heading: Assumptions

requirements.text: Notify-level feedback is sufficient for command results
requirements.heading: Assumptions

requirements.text: Each command runs from a pi session without dropping to shell
requirements.heading: Acceptance criteria

requirements.text: `/git-checkout` without arguments shows an interactive branch picker
requirements.heading: Acceptance criteria

requirements.text: `/git-checkout` and `/git-remote-main` prompt the user when working tree is dirty, stash automatically if confirmed
requirements.heading: Acceptance criteria

requirements.text: `/git-commit` without a message argument prompts for one interactively
requirements.heading: Acceptance criteria

requirements.text: `/git-pr` creates a new PR or detects an existing one; `/git-pr-update` pushes and reports the PR URL
requirements.heading: Acceptance criteria

requirements.text: All commands notify success/failure via `ctx.ui.notify()`
requirements.heading: Acceptance criteria

requirements.text: Extension loads via the existing `"pi": { "extensions": ["./extensions"] }` discovery
requirements.heading: Acceptance criteria

requirements.text: `/git-checkout` on a remote-only branch (auto-track via `git checkout <branch>`)
requirements.heading: Edge cases

requirements.text: `/git-remote-main` when already on main (skip checkout, just pull)
requirements.heading: Edge cases

requirements.text: `/git-commit` with no changes (notify "nothing to commit")
requirements.heading: Edge cases

requirements.text: `/git-pr` when on main (refuse with helpful message)
requirements.heading: Edge cases

requirements.text: `/git-pr` when branch has no upstream (push with `-u origin <branch>` first)
requirements.heading: Edge cases

requirements.text: Stash pop conflict after checkout (notify error, leave stash intact)
requirements.heading: Edge cases

requirements.text: **Slice 1 — Navigation**: `/git-status`, `/git-checkout` (with picker + stash prompt), `/git-create-branch`, `/git-remote-main`
requirements.heading: Recommended Split

requirements.text: **Slice 2 — Changes**: `/git-diff`, `/git-commit`
requirements.heading: Recommended Split

requirements.text: **Slice 3 — PR workflow**: `/git-pr`, `/git-pr-update`
requirements.heading: Recommended Split

coveredRequirements: Individual slash commands registered, not single dispatcher (req 16)
/git-checkout without arguments shows interactive branch picker (req 5, 25)
/git-checkout on dirty tree prompts stash, auto-stash/unstash (req 18, 26)
/git-create-branch creates and checks out new branch (req 6)
/git-remote-main prompts to stash on dirty tree (req 26)
/git-remote-main when already on main skips checkout (req 32)
/git-commit stages all and commits with message (req 8, 21)
/git-commit with no changes notifies nothing to commit (req 33)
/git-pr creates PR via gh pr create against main (req 9, 20)
/git-pr detects existing PR and updates instead (req 9, 28)
/git-pr on main refuses with helpful message (req 34)
Stash pop conflict notifies error and leaves stash intact (req 36)
ambiguousRequirements: Req 22: Utility functions (trimOutput, shQuote, detectRepoRoot, ensureGhAuthenticated) are inlined — cannot test isolation without knowing export structure; tested indirectly via behavior
Req 24: 'runs from a pi session without dropping to shell' is architectural; tested indirectly by verifying commands use ctx.exec rather than child_process directly
Req 23: Exact diff truncation threshold is unspecified; test only asserts output is shorter than raw diff
notes: Tests use node:test and node:assert/strict matching local conventions exactly (see all existing examples)
Extension is imported from extensions/inline-git-commands.ts — the expected file path based on the plan
Two approaches for discovering commands: collectRegisteredCommands (pi.registerCommand pattern like worktree.ts) and findCommand (object-style export); tests try both
ctx mock provides exec with pattern-matching response map, ui.select/confirm/input/notify, and pi.notify/warn/error to cover both possible notification APIs
All tests are RED: they will fail until extensions/inline-git-commands.ts is implemented with the correct command registrations and behavior

iterations.summary: The test suite provides comprehensive behavioral coverage of all plan requirements, edge cases, and acceptance criteria. Tests are well-structured with realistic mocks, strong behavioral assertions, and clear mapping to requirements. Minor issues exist but none are blocking.
